codeReview.summary: Core article detail functionality is implemented, but hit-count behavior is inconsistent with the feature contract: the page increments views on load but does not update the rendered count to match the post-increment DB value. I also noted a hidden-article exposure edge case on the hit endpoint.

issues.description: The page increments hits (`POST /api/articles/:id/hit`) but never updates local `articleHits` state from that response; rendered `Views:` uses the pre-increment value (`article.articleHits`), so UI count can diverge from DB after each view. This conflicts with the requirement that displayed hit count matches article views accounting for current visit increment (VAL-DETAIL-004/005).

`POST /api/articles/:id/hit` increments any existing article ID without checking `articleDisplay = 'y'`. Hidden articles are inaccessible by slug, but their hit counters are still mutable if IDs are known, which weakens the hidden-content boundary intent.

summary: Fail — blocking issue on hit-count correctness: view increments occur but displayed count is stale relative to DB. Non-blocking: hidden-article IDs can still have hit counts incremented via the public hit endpoint.
